text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::The Posture}} is defined as: Pull your shoulders back and down. Expand your chest slightly to project openness.
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: The Posture
Description: Pull your shoulders back and down. Expand your chest slightly to project openness.
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'The Posture' es: Pull your shoulders back and down. Expand your chest slightly to project openness.

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::The Eye Contact}} is defined as: Establish a friendly, 3-second eye contact connection. Avoid staring, but do not look down immediately.
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: The Eye Contact
Description: Establish a friendly, 3-second eye contact connection. Avoid staring, but do not look down immediately.
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'The Eye Contact' es: Establish a friendly, 3-second eye contact connection. Avoid staring, but do not look down immediately.

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::The Greeting}} is defined as: Use a warm, resonant voice. Keep your handshakes firm and match the other person's grip pressure.
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: The Greeting
Description: Use a warm, resonant voice. Keep your handshakes firm and match the other person's grip pressure.
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'The Greeting' es: Use a warm, resonant voice. Keep your handshakes firm and match the other person's grip pressure.

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::Downward Inflection}} is defined as: Stating facts with a descending pitch at the end of sentences conveys authority and certainty.
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: Downward Inflection
Description: Stating facts with a descending pitch at the end of sentences conveys authority and certainty.
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'Downward Inflection' es: Stating facts with a descending pitch at the end of sentences conveys authority and certainty.

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::Avoiding "Uptalk"}} is defined as: Rising pitch at the end of statements makes them sound like questions, conveying doubt and insecurity.
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: Avoiding "Uptalk"
Description: Rising pitch at the end of statements makes them sound like questions, conveying doubt and insecurity.
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'Avoiding "Uptalk"' es: Rising pitch at the end of statements makes them sound like questions, conveying doubt and insecurity.

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::Vocal Resonance}} is defined as: Speak from your chest (chest voice) rather than your throat or head to sound warm and reliable.
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: Vocal Resonance
Description: Speak from your chest (chest voice) rather than your throat or head to sound warm and reliable.
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'Vocal Resonance' es: Speak from your chest (chest voice) rather than your throat or head to sound warm and reliable.

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::Eliminate Minimizers}} is defined as: Remove words like "just" (*"I just wanted to check..."*), "actually" (*"Actually, I think..."*), or "sorry" (*"Sorry to bother you..."*).
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: Eliminate Minimizers
Description: Remove words like "just" (*"I just wanted to check..."*), "actually" (*"Actually, I think..."*), or "sorry" (*"Sorry to bother you..."*).
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'Eliminate Minimizers' es: Remove words like "just" (*"I just wanted to check..."*), "actually" (*"Actually, I think..."*), or "sorry" (*"Sorry to bother you..."*).

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::Own Your Opinion}} is defined as: Replace *"I think this is good"* with *"This is the recommended solution."*
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: Own Your Opinion
Description: Replace *"I think this is good"* with *"This is the recommended solution."*
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'Own Your Opinion' es: Replace *"I think this is good"* with *"This is the recommended solution."*

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::The Filler Word Ban}} is defined as: Eliminate "mmm", "uh", "like", and "so" by replacing them with silent pauses.
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: The Filler Word Ban
Description: Eliminate "mmm", "uh", "like", and "so" by replacing them with silent pauses.
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'The Filler Word Ban' es: Eliminate "mmm", "uh", "like", and "so" by replacing them with silent pauses.

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::Voice Projection}} is defined as: Speak from your diaphragm. Take deep belly breaths, not shallow chest breaths.
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: Voice Projection
Description: Speak from your diaphragm. Take deep belly breaths, not shallow chest breaths.
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'Voice Projection' es: Speak from your diaphragm. Take deep belly breaths, not shallow chest breaths.

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::Claiming Space Early}} is defined as: Make a contribution in the first 10 minutes of a meeting, even if it is just a clarifying question.
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: Claiming Space Early
Description: Make a contribution in the first 10 minutes of a meeting, even if it is just a clarifying question.
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'Claiming Space Early' es: Make a contribution in the first 10 minutes of a meeting, even if it is just a clarifying question.

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::Slow Down your Cadence}} is defined as: High-status individuals speak at a measured, controlled pace (~130-150 words per minute).
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: Slow Down your Cadence
Description: High-status individuals speak at a measured, controlled pace (~130-150 words per minute).
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'Slow Down your Cadence' es: High-status individuals speak at a measured, controlled pace (~130-150 words per minute).

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::The Hook}} is defined as: Start with a surprise, a dramatic question, or a vivid sensory detail. Never start with dry credentials or background.
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: The Hook
Description: Start with a surprise, a dramatic question, or a vivid sensory detail. Never start with dry credentials or background.
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'The Hook' es: Start with a surprise, a dramatic question, or a vivid sensory detail. Never start with dry credentials or background.

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::The Conflict}} is defined as: Clearly outline the obstacle or pain point.
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: The Conflict
Description: Clearly outline the obstacle or pain point.
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'The Conflict' es: Clearly outline the obstacle or pain point.

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::The Resolution}} is defined as: Deliver the lesson or the solution clearly.
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: The Resolution
Description: Deliver the lesson or the solution clearly.
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'The Resolution' es: Deliver the lesson or the solution clearly.

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::Open Gestures}} is defined as: Show your palms (signals honesty and lack of threat).
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: Open Gestures
Description: Show your palms (signals honesty and lack of threat).
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'Open Gestures' es: Show your palms (signals honesty and lack of threat).

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::Relaxed Asymmetry}} is defined as: Avoid standing like a wooden post. Shift your weight naturally to look comfortable.
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: Relaxed Asymmetry
Description: Avoid standing like a wooden post. Shift your weight naturally to look comfortable.
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'Relaxed Asymmetry' es: Avoid standing like a wooden post. Shift your weight naturally to look comfortable.

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::Smile While Speaking}} is defined as: It alters your vocal cords slightly, making your voice sound warmer and more engaging.
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: Smile While Speaking
Description: It alters your vocal cords slightly, making your voice sound warmer and more engaging.
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'Smile While Speaking' es: It alters your vocal cords slightly, making your voice sound warmer and more engaging.

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::The Feet Indicator}} is defined as: If someone's feet are pointed toward the door, they want to leave, regardless of how polite their face is.
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: The Feet Indicator
Description: If someone's feet are pointed toward the door, they want to leave, regardless of how polite their face is.
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'The Feet Indicator' es: If someone's feet are pointed toward the door, they want to leave, regardless of how polite their face is.

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::Micro-expressions}} is defined as: Watch for brief flashes of frustration (tight lips) or skepticism (one raised eyebrow).
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: Micro-expressions
Description: Watch for brief flashes of frustration (tight lips) or skepticism (one raised eyebrow).
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'Micro-expressions' es: Watch for brief flashes of frustration (tight lips) or skepticism (one raised eyebrow).

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::Mirroring}} is defined as: When people are in agreement, they naturally mirror each other's postures.
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: Mirroring
Description: When people are in agreement, they naturally mirror each other's postures.
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'Mirroring' es: When people are in agreement, they naturally mirror each other's postures.

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::SBI Model}} is defined as: *   **Situation**: Describe the specific context (*"During yesterday's client meeting..."*).
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: SBI Model
Description: * **Situation**: Describe the specific context (*"During yesterday's client meeting..."*).
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'SBI Model' es: *   **Situation**: Describe the specific context (*"During yesterday's client meeting..."*).

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::Behavior}} is defined as: Describe the objective action (*"...you interrupted the client twice..."*).
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: Behavior
Description: Describe the objective action (*"...you interrupted the client twice..."*).
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'Behavior' es: Describe the objective action (*"...you interrupted the client twice..."*).

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::Impact}} is defined as: Describe the outcome (*"...which made them defensive and stopped the flow of the pitch."*).
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: Impact
Description: Describe the outcome (*"...which made them defensive and stopped the flow of the pitch."*).
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'Impact' es: Describe the outcome (*"...which made them defensive and stopped the flow of the pitch."*).

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::Collaborate on Solution}} is defined as: *"How can we ensure they have space to finish their slides next time?"*
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: Collaborate on Solution
Description: *"How can we ensure they have space to finish their slides next time?"*
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'Collaborate on Solution' es: *"How can we ensure they have space to finish their slides next time?"*

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::Active De-escalation}} is defined as: Lower your volume and slow down your tempo.
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: Active De-escalation
Description: Lower your volume and slow down your tempo.
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'Active De-escalation' es: Lower your volume and slow down your tempo.

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::Neutral Labeling}} is defined as: Describe the emotion without judgment (*"It seems you are feeling frustrated about this timeline."*).
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: Neutral Labeling
Description: Describe the emotion without judgment (*"It seems you are feeling frustrated about this timeline."*).
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'Neutral Labeling' es: Describe the emotion without judgment (*"It seems you are feeling frustrated about this timeline."*).

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::Selective Agreement}} is defined as: Find a small point you agree on to diffuse tension (*"You are absolutely right that this delay is costly."*).
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: Selective Agreement
Description: Find a small point you agree on to diffuse tension (*"You are absolutely right that this delay is costly."*).
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'Selective Agreement' es: Find a small point you agree on to diffuse tension (*"You are absolutely right that this delay is costly."*).

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::The Validation}} is defined as: Acknowledge the request (*"I appreciate you thinking of me for this proposal."*).
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: The Validation
Description: Acknowledge the request (*"I appreciate you thinking of me for this proposal."*).
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'The Validation' es: Acknowledge the request (*"I appreciate you thinking of me for this proposal."*).

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::The Direct Refusal}} is defined as: Say no without using weak excuses (*"However, I cannot take this on due to my current project commitments."*).
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: The Direct Refusal
Description: Say no without using weak excuses (*"However, I cannot take this on due to my current project commitments."*).
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'The Direct Refusal' es: Say no without using weak excuses (*"However, I cannot take this on due to my current project commitments."*).

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::The Alternative}} is defined as: Offer an alternative (*"I can review the final draft next Thursday, or you can ask Sarah who has capacity."*).
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: The Alternative
Description: Offer an alternative (*"I can review the final draft next Thursday, or you can ask Sarah who has capacity."*).
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'The Alternative' es: Offer an alternative (*"I can review the final draft next Thursday, or you can ask Sarah who has capacity."*).

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::The Bridging Technique}} is defined as: Use the speaker's last words to launch your point.
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: The Bridging Technique
Description: Use the speaker's last words to launch your point.
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'The Bridging Technique' es: Use the speaker's last words to launch your point.

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::Bridging Phrases}} is defined as: *   *"Building on that point, we should also consider..."*
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: Bridging Phrases
Description: * *"Building on that point, we should also consider..."*
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'Bridging Phrases' es: *   *"Building on that point, we should also consider..."*

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::The Hand Gesture}} is defined as: Raise your hand slightly to signal you have something to add.
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: The Hand Gesture
Description: Raise your hand slightly to signal you have something to add.
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'The Hand Gesture' es: Raise your hand slightly to signal you have something to add.

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::Tongue Drills}} is defined as: Warm up your mouth before speaking.
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: Tongue Drills
Description: Warm up your mouth before speaking.
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'Tongue Drills' es: Warm up your mouth before speaking.

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::Vocal Fry}} is defined as: Avoid the croaky sound at the end of sentences by supporting your breath.
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: Vocal Fry
Description: Avoid the croaky sound at the end of sentences by supporting your breath.
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'Vocal Fry' es: Avoid the croaky sound at the end of sentences by supporting your breath.

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::The Articulation Test}} is defined as: Say tongue twisters daily to train your jaw, lips, and tongue.
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: The Articulation Test
Description: Say tongue twisters daily to train your jaw, lips, and tongue.
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'The Articulation Test' es: Say tongue twisters daily to train your jaw, lips, and tongue.

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::Reflecting}} is defined as: Repeat back key words (*"So what you're saying is the codebase is unstable?"*).
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: Reflecting
Description: Repeat back key words (*"So what you're saying is the codebase is unstable?"*).
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'Reflecting' es: Repeat back key words (*"So what you're saying is the codebase is unstable?"*).

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::Summarizing}} is defined as: Wrap up their points (*"To make sure I understand: we need to fix the database before we launch the UI?"*).
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: Summarizing
Description: Wrap up their points (*"To make sure I understand: we need to fix the database before we launch the UI?"*).
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'Summarizing' es: Wrap up their points (*"To make sure I understand: we need to fix the database before we launch the UI?"*).

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::Open Clarifiers}} is defined as: Ask *"What does success look like for this project?"* instead of *"Is this project good?"*
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: Open Clarifiers
Description: Ask *"What does success look like for this project?"* instead of *"Is this project good?"*
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'Open Clarifiers' es: Ask *"What does success look like for this project?"* instead of *"Is this project good?"*

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::Ban the Scripts}} is defined as: Never ask "How are you?" or "What do you do?".
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: Ban the Scripts
Description: Never ask "How are you?" or "What do you do?".
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'Ban the Scripts' es: Never ask "How are you?" or "What do you do?".

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::The Magic Prompts}} is defined as: *   *"What has been the highlight of your week?"*
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: The Magic Prompts
Description: * *"What has been the highlight of your week?"*
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'The Magic Prompts' es: *   *"What has been the highlight of your week?"*

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::The Thread Hook}} is defined as: Listen for hobbies or locations they mention, and ask about them.
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: The Thread Hook
Description: Listen for hobbies or locations they mention, and ask about them.
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'The Thread Hook' es: Listen for hobbies or locations they mention, and ask about them.

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::Poise}} is defined as: Take a breath. Do not explain yourself immediately.
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: Poise
Description: Take a breath. Do not explain yourself immediately.
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'Poise' es: Take a breath. Do not explain yourself immediately.

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::The Clarifying Prompt}} is defined as: *"Thank you for pointing that out. Could you share a specific example so I can make sure I understand?"*
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: The Clarifying Prompt
Description: *"Thank you for pointing that out. Could you share a specific example so I can make sure I understand?"*
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'The Clarifying Prompt' es: *"Thank you for pointing that out. Could you share a specific example so I can make sure I understand?"*

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::The Commitment}} is defined as: *"I appreciate the feedback. I will adjust this in the next iteration."*
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: The Commitment
Description: *"I appreciate the feedback. I will adjust this in the next iteration."*
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'The Commitment' es: *"I appreciate the feedback. I will adjust this in the next iteration."*

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::Threading}} is defined as: Identify keywords in the other person's sentences and hook your next sentence onto them.
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: Threading
Description: Identify keywords in the other person's sentences and hook your next sentence onto them.
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'Threading' es: Identify keywords in the other person's sentences and hook your next sentence onto them.

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::The Pre-Emphasis Pause}} is defined as: Pause for 1 second *before* you say a key word to build anticipation.
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: The Pre-Emphasis Pause
Description: Pause for 1 second *before* you say a key word to build anticipation.
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'The Pre-Emphasis Pause' es: Pause for 1 second *before* you say a key word to build anticipation.

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::The Post-Statement Pause}} is defined as: Pause for 2 seconds *after* an important point to let it sink in.
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: The Post-Statement Pause
Description: Pause for 2 seconds *after* an important point to let it sink in.
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'The Post-Statement Pause' es: Pause for 2 seconds *after* an important point to let it sink in.

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::The Question Pause}} is defined as: Pause before answering a question to show you are thinking, not just reacting.
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: The Question Pause
Description: Pause before answering a question to show you are thinking, not just reacting.
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'The Question Pause' es: Pause before answering a question to show you are thinking, not just reacting.

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::Focus on Interests, Not Positions}} is defined as: Ask *"What goal are we trying to achieve here?"* rather than defending your method.
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: Focus on Interests, Not Positions
Description: Ask *"What goal are we trying to achieve here?"* rather than defending your method.
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'Focus on Interests, Not Positions' es: Ask *"What goal are we trying to achieve here?"* rather than defending your method.

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::Shared Objectives}} is defined as: Emphasize alignment (*"We both want to launch a stable app by October."*).
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: Shared Objectives
Description: Emphasize alignment (*"We both want to launch a stable app by October."*).
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'Shared Objectives' es: Emphasize alignment (*"We both want to launch a stable app by October."*).

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::Avoid Blame Language}} is defined as: Use "I" statements (*"I feel our current timeline is risky"*) instead of "You" statements (*"You are pushing a risky timeline"*).
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: Avoid Blame Language
Description: Use "I" statements (*"I feel our current timeline is risky"*) instead of "You" statements (*"You are pushing a risky timeline"*).
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'Avoid Blame Language' es: Use "I" statements (*"I feel our current timeline is risky"*) instead of "You" statements (*"You are pushing a risky timeline"*).

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::The Camera Eye}} is defined as: Look directly at the camera lens, not at the screen. This simulates eye contact for the audience.
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: The Camera Eye
Description: Look directly at the camera lens, not at the screen. This simulates eye contact for the audience.
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'The Camera Eye' es: Look directly at the camera lens, not at the screen. This simulates eye contact for the audience.

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::Body Framing}} is defined as: Position your camera so your head and chest are visible, allowing people to see your hand gestures.
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: Body Framing
Description: Position your camera so your head and chest are visible, allowing people to see your hand gestures.
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'Body Framing' es: Position your camera so your head and chest are visible, allowing people to see your hand gestures.

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::Dynamic Volume}} is defined as: Speak with 10% more energy and vocal variance than you would in person to compensate for screen fatigue.
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: Dynamic Volume
Description: Speak with 10% more energy and vocal variance than you would in person to compensate for screen fatigue.
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'Dynamic Volume' es: Speak with 10% more energy and vocal variance than you would in person to compensate for screen fatigue.

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::The Sticky Pitch Structure}} is defined as: 1.  **The Hook**: A compelling stat or story (*"Eighty percent of users churn on day one."*).
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: The Sticky Pitch Structure
Description: 1. **The Hook**: A compelling stat or story (*"Eighty percent of users churn on day one."*).
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'The Sticky Pitch Structure' es: 1.  **The Hook**: A compelling stat or story (*"Eighty percent of users churn on day one."*).

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::The Problem}} is defined as: The core issue (*"This happens because our onboarding flow is too complex."*).
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: The Problem
Description: The core issue (*"This happens because our onboarding flow is too complex."*).
spanish: En la fonética de SoftSkills, la regla de 'The Problem' es: The core issue (*"This happens because our onboarding flow is too complex."*).

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::The Solution}} is defined as: Your idea (*"We should implement a 3-step interactive setup wizard."*).
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.

Rule: The Solution
Description: Your idea (*"We should implement a 3-step interactive setup wizard."*).

text: In SoftSkills phonetics, the rule of {{c1::The Benefit}} is defined as: The ROI (*"By doing this, we can increase day-one retention by twenty percent."*).
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for SoftSkills.
usage:
Rule: The Benefit
Description: The ROI (*"By doing this, we can increase day-one retention by twenty percent."*).

text: What is the SoftSkills pronunciation shortcut/tip for {{c1::The Tri-Point Connection}}? When entering a room, look at: (1) the person's eyes, (2) their smile/mouth, (3) their body orientation. Align your chest toward the person to signal focus and respect.
explanation: Pronunciation tip/shortcut for SoftSkills.
usage:
Tip: The Tri-Point Connection
When entering a room, look at: (1) the person's eyes, (2) their smile/mouth, (3) their body orientation. Align your chest toward the person to signal focus and respect.
spanish: ¿Cuál es el atajo/consejo de pronunciación de SoftSkills para 'The Tri-Point Connection'? When entering a room, look at: (1) the person's eyes, (2) their smile/mouth, (3) their body orientation. Align your chest toward the person to signal focus and respect.

text: What is the SoftSkills pronunciation shortcut/tip for {{c1::The Trust Anchor}}? End your key points with a firm, dropping pitch. Instead of asking "Does that make sense? ↗" state "That is the optimal approach for our timeline. ↘"
explanation: Pronunciation tip/shortcut for SoftSkills.
usage:
Tip: The Trust Anchor
End your key points with a firm, dropping pitch. Instead of asking "Does that make sense? ↗" state "That is the optimal approach for our timeline. ↘"
spanish: ¿Cuál es el atajo/consejo de pronunciación de SoftSkills para 'The Trust Anchor'? End your key points with a firm, dropping pitch. Instead of asking "Does that make sense? ↗" state "That is the optimal approach for our timeline. ↘"

text: What is the SoftSkills pronunciation shortcut/tip for {{c1::The Sorry Swap}}? If you need to point out a mistake, don't start with "Sorry." Swap "Sorry for the delay" to "Thank you for your patience." Swap "Sorry to interrupt" to "I'd like to add a quick point here."
explanation: Pronunciation tip/shortcut for SoftSkills.
usage:
Tip: The Sorry Swap
If you need to point out a mistake, don't start with "Sorry." Swap "Sorry for the delay" to "Thank you for your patience." Swap "Sorry to interrupt" to "I'd like to add a quick point here."
spanish: ¿Cuál es el atajo/consejo de pronunciación de SoftSkills para 'The Sorry Swap'? If you need to point out a mistake, don't start with "Sorry." Swap "Sorry for the delay" to "Thank you for your patience." Swap "Sorry to interrupt" to "I'd like to add a quick point here."

text: What is the SoftSkills pronunciation shortcut/tip for {{c1::The Diaphragm Warm-Up}}? Place your hand on your stomach. Say "Ha! Ha! Ha!" pushing your stomach outward with each breath. Feel the vibration in your belly. This is where your speaking power comes from.
explanation: Pronunciation tip/shortcut for SoftSkills.
usage:
Tip: The Diaphragm Warm-Up
Place your hand on your stomach. Say "Ha! Ha! Ha!" pushing your stomach outward with each breath. Feel the vibration in your belly. This is where your speaking power comes from.
spanish: ¿Cuál es el atajo/consejo de pronunciación de SoftSkills para 'The Diaphragm Warm-Up'? Place your hand on your stomach. Say "Ha! Ha! Ha!" pushing your stomach outward with each breath. Feel the vibration in your belly. This is where your speaking power comes from.

text: What is the SoftSkills pronunciation shortcut/tip for {{c1::The In-Media-Res Hook}}? Start your stories in the middle of the action. Instead of saying "We had a project last year that went bad," say "It was 11 PM on a Friday, and our main server had just crashed."
explanation: Pronunciation tip/shortcut for SoftSkills.
usage:
Tip: The In-Media-Res Hook
Start your stories in the middle of the action. Instead of saying "We had a project last year that went bad," say "It was 11 PM on a Friday, and our main server had just crashed."
spanish: ¿Cuál es el atajo/consejo de pronunciación de SoftSkills para 'The In-Media-Res Hook'? Start your stories in the middle of the action. Instead of saying "We had a project last year that went bad," say "It was 11 PM on a Friday, and our main server had just crashed."

text: What is the SoftSkills pronunciation shortcut/tip for {{c1::The Warmth Ratio}}? In casual talk, use the 80/20 rule: 80% warm, open body language, 20% firm professional focus. Smile when greeting, but keep your face neutral when delivering serious business metrics.
explanation: Pronunciation tip/shortcut for SoftSkills.
usage:
Tip: The Warmth Ratio
In casual talk, use the 80/20 rule: 80% warm, open body language, 20% firm professional focus. Smile when greeting, but keep your face neutral when delivering serious business metrics.
spanish: ¿Cuál es el atajo/consejo de pronunciación de SoftSkills para 'The Warmth Ratio'? In casual talk, use the 80/20 rule: 80% warm, open body language, 20% firm professional focus. Smile when greeting, but keep your face neutral when delivering serious business metrics.

text: What is the SoftSkills pronunciation shortcut/tip for {{c1::The Speech Rate Match}}? Match the speech rate and volume of your listener. If they speak slowly and quietly, lower your volume and slow your tempo to build rapport.
explanation: Pronunciation tip/shortcut for SoftSkills.
usage:
Tip: The Speech Rate Match
Match the speech rate and volume of your listener. If they speak slowly and quietly, lower your volume and slow your tempo to build rapport.
spanish: ¿Cuál es el atajo/consejo de pronunciación de SoftSkills para 'The Speech Rate Match'? Match the speech rate and volume of your listener. If they speak slowly and quietly, lower your volume and slow your tempo to build rapport.

text: What is the SoftSkills pronunciation shortcut/tip for {{c1::The Feedback Rule}}? Keep feedback under 2 minutes. Deliver the SBI points directly, then pause and let the other person respond. Do not over-explain.
explanation: Pronunciation tip/shortcut for SoftSkills.
usage:
Tip: The Feedback Rule
Keep feedback under 2 minutes. Deliver the SBI points directly, then pause and let the other person respond. Do not over-explain.
spanish: ¿Cuál es el atajo/consejo de pronunciación de SoftSkills para 'The Feedback Rule'? Keep feedback under 2 minutes. Deliver the SBI points directly, then pause and let the other person respond. Do not over-explain.

text: What is the SoftSkills pronunciation shortcut/tip for {{c1::The Silence Brake}}? When someone yells or speaks aggressively, wait 3 full seconds before replying. The silence forces them to reflect on their own behavior.
explanation: Pronunciation tip/shortcut for SoftSkills.
usage:
Tip: The Silence Brake
When someone yells or speaks aggressively, wait 3 full seconds before replying. The silence forces them to reflect on their own behavior.
spanish: ¿Cuál es el atajo/consejo de pronunciación de SoftSkills para 'The Silence Brake'? When someone yells or speaks aggressively, wait 3 full seconds before replying. The silence forces them to reflect on their own behavior.

text: What is the SoftSkills pronunciation shortcut/tip for {{c1::The Excuse Trap}}? Never give too many reasons for saying no. The more excuses you give, the more room the other person has to negotiate. Keep your refusal short and direct.
explanation: Pronunciation tip/shortcut for SoftSkills.
usage:
Tip: The Excuse Trap
Never give too many reasons for saying no. The more excuses you give, the more room the other person has to negotiate. Keep your refusal short and direct.
spanish: ¿Cuál es el atajo/consejo de pronunciación de SoftSkills para 'The Excuse Trap'? Never give too many reasons for saying no. The more excuses you give, the more room the other person has to negotiate. Keep your refusal short and direct.

text: What is the SoftSkills pronunciation shortcut/tip for {{c1::The Momentum Ride}}? Wait for the speaker to take a breath or pause for emphasis, then immediately start with your bridging phrase in a warm, confident tone.
explanation: Pronunciation tip/shortcut for SoftSkills.
usage:
Tip: The Momentum Ride
Wait for the speaker to take a breath or pause for emphasis, then immediately start with your bridging phrase in a warm, confident tone.
spanish: ¿Cuál es el atajo/consejo de pronunciación de SoftSkills para 'The Momentum Ride'? Wait for the speaker to take a breath or pause for emphasis, then immediately start with your bridging phrase in a warm, confident tone.

text: What is the SoftSkills pronunciation shortcut/tip for {{c1::Articulation Drills}}? Practice these three times before any important presentation: 1. *Red leather, yellow leather, red leather, yellow leather.* (Focus on tongue position) 2. *Unique New York. You know you need unique New York.* (Focus on crisp consonant endings) 3. *Peter Piper picked a peck of pickled peppers.* (Focus on plosive breath control)
explanation: Pronunciation tip/shortcut for SoftSkills.
usage:
Tip: Articulation Drills
Practice these three times before any important presentation: 1. *Red leather, yellow leather, red leather, yellow leather.* (Focus on tongue position) 2. *Unique New York. You know you need unique New York.* (Focus on crisp consonant endings) 3. *Peter Piper picked a peck of pickled peppers.* (Focus on plosive breath control)
spanish: ¿Cuál es el atajo/consejo de pronunciación de SoftSkills para 'Articulation Drills'? Practice these three times before any important presentation: 1. *Red leather, yellow leather, red leather, yellow leather.* (Focus on tongue position) 2. *Unique New York. You know you need unique New York.* (Focus on crisp consonant endings) 3. *Peter Piper picked a peck of pickled peppers.* (Focus on plosive breath control)

text: What is the SoftSkills pronunciation shortcut/tip for {{c1::The 70/30 Rule}}? In conversations, listen 70% of the time and speak 30%. When you do speak, focus on asking deep, open questions rather than sharing your own stories.
explanation: Pronunciation tip/shortcut for SoftSkills.
usage:
Tip: The 70/30 Rule
In conversations, listen 70% of the time and speak 30%. When you do speak, focus on asking deep, open questions rather than sharing your own stories.
spanish: ¿Cuál es el atajo/consejo de pronunciación de SoftSkills para 'The 70/30 Rule'? In conversations, listen 70% of the time and speak 30%. When you do speak, focus on asking deep, open questions rather than sharing your own stories.

text: What is the SoftSkills pronunciation shortcut/tip for {{c1::The "Tell Me More" Hack}}? When someone gives a short answer, say "Tell me more about that." It signals deep interest and prompts them to share richer details.
explanation: Pronunciation tip/shortcut for SoftSkills.
usage:
Tip: The "Tell Me More" Hack
When someone gives a short answer, say "Tell me more about that." It signals deep interest and prompts them to share richer details.
spanish: ¿Cuál es el atajo/consejo de pronunciación de SoftSkills para 'The "Tell Me More" Hack'? When someone gives a short answer, say "Tell me more about that." It signals deep interest and prompts them to share richer details.

text: What is the SoftSkills pronunciation shortcut/tip for {{c1::The Shield Technique}}? View criticism as data about your work, not an attack on your character. Separate your identity from your output.
explanation: Pronunciation tip/shortcut for SoftSkills.
usage:
Tip: The Shield Technique
View criticism as data about your work, not an attack on your character. Separate your identity from your output.
spanish: ¿Cuál es el atajo/consejo de pronunciación de SoftSkills para 'The Shield Technique'? View criticism as data about your work, not an attack on your character. Separate your identity from your output.

text: What is the SoftSkills pronunciation shortcut/tip for {{c1::The Word Hook}}? Listen closely to nouns and adjectives. Every noun is a doorway to a new conversation topic. Pick the one you are most curious about.
explanation: Pronunciation tip/shortcut for SoftSkills.
usage:
Tip: The Word Hook
Listen closely to nouns and adjectives. Every noun is a doorway to a new conversation topic. Pick the one you are most curious about.
spanish: ¿Cuál es el atajo/consejo de pronunciación de SoftSkills para 'The Word Hook'? Listen closely to nouns and adjectives. Every noun is a doorway to a new conversation topic. Pick the one you are most curious about.

text: What is the SoftSkills pronunciation shortcut/tip for {{c1::The Silence Scale}}? Practice speaking with pauses. Say: "We have two options. [Pause 1-2-3] We can adapt, [Pause 1-2-3] or we can fail." Feel how the silence amplifies the weight of your words.
explanation: Pronunciation tip/shortcut for SoftSkills.
usage:
Tip: The Silence Scale
Practice speaking with pauses. Say: "We have two options. [Pause 1-2-3] We can adapt, [Pause 1-2-3] or we can fail." Feel how the silence amplifies the weight of your words.
spanish: ¿Cuál es el atajo/consejo de pronunciación de SoftSkills para 'The Silence Scale'? Practice speaking with pauses. Say: "We have two options. [Pause 1-2-3] We can adapt, [Pause 1-2-3] or we can fail." Feel how the silence amplifies the weight of your words.

text: What is the SoftSkills pronunciation shortcut/tip for {{c1::The Third Chair}}? Position the problem as an external object. Stand or sit side-by-side with your colleague and look at the whiteboard or document together. You are team members fighting the problem, not fighting each other.
explanation: Pronunciation tip/shortcut for SoftSkills.
usage:
Tip: The Third Chair
Position the problem as an external object. Stand or sit side-by-side with your colleague and look at the whiteboard or document together. You are team members fighting the problem, not fighting each other.
spanish: ¿Cuál es el atajo/consejo de pronunciación de SoftSkills para 'The Third Chair'? Position the problem as an external object. Stand or sit side-by-side with your colleague and look at the whiteboard or document together. You are team members fighting the problem, not fighting each other.

text: What is the SoftSkills pronunciation shortcut/tip for {{c1::The Camera Sticky Note}}? Draw a smiley face on a sticky note and paste it right next to your web camera lens. It will remind you to look up and smile naturally throughout virtual meetings.
explanation: Pronunciation tip/shortcut for SoftSkills.
usage:
Tip: The Camera Sticky Note
Draw a smiley face on a sticky note and paste it right next to your web camera lens. It will remind you to look up and smile naturally throughout virtual meetings.
spanish: ¿Cuál es el atajo/consejo de pronunciación de SoftSkills para 'The Camera Sticky Note'? Draw a smiley face on a sticky note and paste it right next to your web camera lens. It will remind you to look up and smile naturally throughout virtual meetings.

text: What is the SoftSkills pronunciation shortcut/tip for {{c1::The Elevator Test}}? Can you pitch your idea in 30 seconds? Practice condensing your project pitch into exactly four sentences matching the Hook-Problem-Solution-Benefit structure.
explanation: Pronunciation tip/shortcut for SoftSkills.
usage:
Tip: The Elevator Test
Can you pitch your idea in 30 seconds? Practice condensing your project pitch into exactly four sentences matching the Hook-Problem-Solution-Benefit structure.
